coaching_note: Return week after 2.5 off — all loads cut ~10–15%, RIR 2–3, zero PR chasing; back to prior loads next week if pain-free. Tendon first: neutral-grip pressing only, d11 stays out, stop any set at the first tendon signal. Lead LEFT on all single-leg/arm work. Do the monster walks and the RDL — they keep getting cut. Get one baseline Athletic day in this week.

a5.note: Lead LEFT every set. The standing leg is doing the stability work — that's the point.

a6.note: Medium band, hips stacked, toes forward. Don't rush these.

a7.note: Same load. BRACE before each rep — left-side low-back soreness last time = hinge form, not load. Soft standing knee.

a8.note: Down from 40 for the return week. 3-sec eccentric. Back to 40 next week if clean.

a10.note: Eccentric-only, 2x5, ~30deg range. Rebuild slowly — hamstring tendons lose tolerance fastest during time off.

a11.note: TKE — 2-sec hold at full extension. Don't skip (missed last session).

a12.note: Step-down 4-sec lower, knee over 2nd toe. Don't skip (missed last session).

b4.note: Down from 110 for return week. All 4 sets clean at 100 → back to 110 next week.

b5.note: Down from 110. Lead left, full retraction.

b6.note: YOUR REQUEST: drop to 35 and ELBOWS OUT (wide) — it's a rear-delt row, not a lat row. Grind reps at 45 with elbows in defeats the purpose.

b7.note: Down from 55. 2-sec hold at the ears.

b8.note: Down from 70. Machine only reaches shoulder height — fine, start there; do the lat stretch (b10) right after for the overhead range.

b9.note: Stay 20. Brace the core (back twinge at 25). Seated if it helps.

c5.note: Down from 80 for return week. 3-sec eccentric, stay at/under 90deg, lead left. Back to 80 next week if pain-free.

c6.note: Down from 50. 2-sec hold at top, toe slightly out, lead left.

c7.note: 18in bodyweight. Priority is killing the left-knee wobble — slow is the goal, not load.

c8.note: 60deg x 40s x3. Hold the depth you had — don't go deeper on the return week.

c9.note: Bodyweight this week. Add 10s/hand next week if the knee is quiet.

c10.note: Yellow band, 3x12 steps/side. This is the fix for the left-knee wobble — treat it as a main lift, not an afterthought.

c11.note: Conventional DB RDL, 20/hand. Don't skip and don't sub — it keeps getting cut for time; move it before c10 if needed.

d4.note: Down from 32.5 — you felt the tendon 'very slightly' here. Do it FIRST in the block, fresh. Stop the set at any tendon signal.

d7.note: Drop to 10 and fix the left shrug — long neck, lead with the elbow. Load comes after control.

d8.note: Converging machine, NEUTRAL grip only, combined weight. Down from 40 — tendon tolerance drops during time off even when strength doesn't.

d9.note: Lead LEFT (weaker side), match reps right to left.

d10.note: Down from 45. Pain-free here historically — this can progress again next week.

d11.note: STILL OMITTED — tendon twinged both attempts. Reintroduce only after 2+ weeks of pain-free pressing. Use the Skipped/Pain flag.

e4.note: First Athletic day ever logged? Go light everywhere — baseline session. Goblet squat 4-sec down, explode up, pain-free depth only.

e7.note: Mini-bands work here. Most ski-specific pattern in the program. Toes forward, full tripod.

e9.note: Lateral step-up 4-6in, slow lower. Baseline only.
